body:
## Sourcing triage — INDO — 2026-07-18 (Sunda / ANALYST_INDO)

Blank-slate book (0 positions). Kill-ledger revisit scan first: DSNG.JK/ELSA.JK/PNBN.JK were Judge-KILLed 2026-07-13 (judge_kill_cooldown_days=60, live until ~2026-09-11) — excluded from consideration regardless of price. All 3 now sit ABOVE their old entry levels anyway (DSNG 1215 vs 1170 entry; ELSA 640 vs 620; PNBN 915 vs 880), so the mechanical "at/below prior entry math" revisit test doesn't even apply to them this week; moot given the cooldown regardless. No watchlist entries exist. Foreign-flow feed remains STALE (0 fresh readings) — flow_z60 ignored everywhere. Screen: data/cache/screen_INDO_2026-07-18.csv (36 rows, WASHOUT/NEGLECTED/MOMENTUM).

### Names re-considered from last week's 2026-07-11 triage (pass reasoning re-checked, not re-litigated from scratch)
- SGRO.JK (NEGLECTED, CPO) — still the deepest washout in the book (off -56.62%) but ADV ~428mn IDR, essentially unchanged from last week's ~392mn IDR — the "10x thinner than DSNG, real ARA/ARB unsizeability risk" pass reason is unchanged (and DSNG is gone this week anyway). PASS again, monitor-only.
- AADI.JK (MOMENTUM, coal) — same "macro/China-import-policy edge, MACRO_FX's lane not this book's" reasoning still applies. PASS again.
- RALS.JK (NEGLECTED, consumer) — still flattish (off -18.49%, mom12 +7.93%), no compensating mispricing story. PASS again.
- AALI.JK (WASHOUT, CPO) — still positive mom12 (+12.69%) questioning the washout tag, and weaker cagr than LSIP (+11.97% vs LSIP's +37.03%) within the same CPO cluster. PASS again.
- GOTO.JK (WASHOUT, tech) — cagr still 0.0 (no earnings track record), heavy coverage, Rp50 floor-adjacent price, same tick-size/board-lot caveat. PASS again.
- GGRM.JK (MOMENTUM, tobacco) — price-vs-fundamentals gap has widened further (mom12 now +87.32% vs cagr +5.91%, versus +73.8%/+2.7% last week). PASS again, more strongly.
- MAPI.JK (MOMENTUM, consumer) — still near 52w highs (-0.98%) with a weak cagr relative to the move. PASS again.
- ADRO.JK (MOMENTUM, coal) — still liquid/trending, heavy coverage, no washout component. PASS again.
- LSIP.JK (NEGLECTED #4/MOMENTUM #7, CPO) — REVISITED, now a PICK. Last week's pass reason was "least washed-out CPO name, mispricing already closed vs DSNG/SGRO" — but DSNG is now cooldown-excluded and SGRO remains unsizeable on liquidity, so LSIP is the best available CPO exposure this book has this week by elimination, and on its own merits it screens better than the other surviving CPO names (cagr +37.03% vs AALI's +11.97%, similar off-high depth to AALI at workable ADV ~10.9bn IDR).
- JPFA.JK (WASHOUT #1/NEGLECTED #11, poultry) — was runner-up/swap-in last week for a dossier-stage stumble, not a full-cycle kill; treating fresh this week rather than auto-promoting. Thin coverage, continued washout-with-recovery pattern (off -25.25%, mom12 +35.69%). No clean method-card fit among the 10 house cards (protein/feed producer isn't a plantation/commodity-cyclical in the CPO sense, isn't a bank/infra/holdco, and isn't a clean quality-compounder without a moat argument yet to be built) — deferred, not killed; flagging as next swap-in candidate.

### New names this week (not in 2026-07-11 screen)
1. JSMR.JK (WASHOUT #7, toll roads, infra_towers/domestic_cycle, off -28.77%, cagr -24.93% — worst momentum in the book, moderate coverage, ADV ~24.4bn IDR) — PICK. VAL_INFRA_TOWERS_TELCO explicit fit (toll roads named in the card); genuinely fresh sector for this desk.
2. ASII.JK (WASHOUT #6, auto/conglomerate, domestic_cycle/coal_china, off -27.6%, mega/heavy coverage, ADV ~304bn IDR — best liquidity in the book) — PICK. Textbook SOTP holdco (listed auto distribution/manufacturing + heavy-equipment/mining contracting + agribusiness + financial services + infra stakes). Assigned VAL_HOLDCO_DISCOUNT; per that card's own rule a dated catalyst is mandatory or the thesis routes to EVENT_SPECIAL_SIT/pass — not pre-judging that at sourcing stage, leaving it to the analyst's phase-1/2 work and Bear/Judge to enforce.
3. TLKM.JK (WASHOUT #2, telecom, idr_defensive/em_flows, off -26.98%, mega/heavy coverage, ADV ~438bn IDR) — PASS (close). Same VAL_INFRA_TOWERS_TELCO card as JSMR; picking both dilutes construction-chain sector breadth versus adding JSMR's toll-road angle alongside ASII's holdco and LSIP's commodity-cyclical picks. First swap-in if JSMR falters in dossier stage.
4. MYOR.JK / CMRY.JK (WASHOUT, consumer) — PASS. Moderate/thin coverage but no compensating washout-depth or mispricing story beyond generic consumer-cycle softness already priced by the whole WASHOUT sleeve.
5. PWON.JK (WASHOUT, property) — PASS. BI's defensive hiking cycle (state/macro_regime.md) is a direct headwind for property-rates-sensitive names; no thesis here fights the macro regime call without a name-specific offset not yet identified.
6. INTP.JK, MTEL.JK, SIDO.JK (WASHOUT) — PASS, uniformly. No differentiated angle over the three picks above; SIDO's cagr -24.18% and MTEL's -15.64% mirror generic domestic-cycle softness already captured by JSMR/ASII.
7. ERAA.JK (WASHOUT, consumer electronics retail, thin coverage) — PASS (close), swap-in candidate alongside JPFA if a pick falters.
8. BJTM.JK (NEGLECTED, regional bank) — PASS. Off only -3.84% from high — not actually washed out; VAL_BANKS needs a real dislocation to be worth the LK-footnote digging this desk already did on PNBN (now killed) — no new information edge over that exercise.
9. TAPG.JK (NEGLECTED/MOMENTUM, CPO) — PASS. cagr +94.26% and mom12 +76.3% — already re-rated hard, opposite of a washout entry.
10. MAIN.JK, LPPF.JK, AUTO.JK (NEGLECTED, consumer) — PASS. Thin coverage but flattish/no compensating mispricing story, same class as RALS.
11. DMAS.JK, ITMG.JK, AKRA.JK, PRDA.JK, BBTN.JK (MOMENTUM) — PASS, uniformly. None offer a washout/neglected entry; all chasing an existing move, several (ITMG, coal) also overlapping AADI's macro-not-primary-source pass reasoning.

### Final 3 picks for deep-dive (step 7)
- **JSMR.JK** (WASHOUT) — edge claimed: analytical (toll-road traffic/tariff-adjustment data under-modeled at moderate coverage vs the domestic-cycle-softness narrative priced into the whole sleeve).
- **ASII.JK** (WASHOUT) — edge claimed: analytical (look-through SOTP across auto/heavy-equipment/agribusiness/financial-services segments vs a single blended conglomerate multiple; catalyst-or-pass discipline applies at construction).
- **LSIP.JK** (NEGLECTED) — edge claimed: analytical (FFB yield/replanting capex read within the CPO cluster, best surviving mispricing-to-liquidity ratio now that DSNG is cooldown-excluded).

All 3 non-MOMENTUM (2 WASHOUT + 1 NEGLECTED); rule satisfied trivially. No dossiers/thesis files written at this stage.

Portfolio review (Kretek, PM_INDO) — INDO book: 0 positions, 0% gross, 100% cash, drawdown 0%, governor NONE. No holding-level distance-to-kill/target review applicable (nothing held). No market-value concentration (>15% NAV) possible at 0 names. Foreign-flow feed remains STALE — flagged, not actionable against a holding that doesn't exist. Nothing to re-affirm this week; construction pipeline (step 7) is this book's only active work.

ADVANCE: TLKM.JK (WASHOUT #2, BI-tightening telco drawdown, EV +8.75-9.0%), ADRO.JK (MOMENTUM #3, coal momentum + dividend anchor, EV +9.0%). WATCH: PWON.JK (WASHOUT #5, property developer, EV +1.6% borderline, BI headwind — needs rate pivot signal). NOT CARDED: ASII.JK/DSNG.JK/ELSA.JK/JSMR.JK/LSIP.JK/PNBN.JK (existing theses), MAPI.JK/GGRM.JK (thin moats, wait for fundamental inflection). Session state: 100% cash, 6 judged-unticketed theses pending next trading session.

meta.title: CONTAMINATION — incidental price-derived data during Phase 1

body:
Per constitution section 3 (consensus quarantine): "An agent meeting banned data logs a persistent CONTAMINATION journal entry and discards it."

During Phase-1 (fundamentals-only) research on T-INDO-LSIP-001 (PP London Sumatra), an early WebSearch results summary surfaced a trailing PE ratio (4.73), forward PE (5.12), and a market-cap figure (IDR 8.93tn) before the analyst restricted subsequent searches to primary-source domains. These are price-derived, market-implied numbers banned from the Phase-1 context per the two-phase analyst protocol.

Disposition: the analyst discarded these figures immediately upon recognizing them and confirmed none appear anywhere in the NAV/estimate work committed to the thesis file — all subsequent research used company primary filings (annual report/financial statements) and public commodity-price series only. No re-work needed; no committed estimate, archetype selection, or base_rate anchor was influenced by the leaked figures.

meta.title: CONTAMINATION — incidental price-derived data during Phase 1

body:
Per constitution section 3 (consensus quarantine): "An agent meeting banned data logs a persistent CONTAMINATION journal entry and discards it."

During Phase-1 (fundamentals-only) research on T-INDO-ASII-001 (Astra International), a WebSearch query on Astra's dividend policy surfaced an aggregator snippet referencing "dividend yields between 1.3-1.9%". Dividend yield is a price-derived, market-implied number, banned from the Phase-1 context per the two-phase analyst protocol (no prices, no market-implied numbers) even though it is not itself a sell-side rating/price-target/recommendation-count.

Disposition: the analyst did not use, cite, or anchor any part of the thesis on this figure — only IDR-per-share dividend amounts and payout-ratio percentages (non-price-derived) were used in the Phase-1 build. The number is discarded per protocol; logging here per the constitution's mandatory-log requirement. No re-work needed since the figure was never incorporated into any committed estimate or the base_rate/archetype selection.

## Sourcing triage — GLOBAL — 2026-07-18 (Sloane / ANALYST_GLOBAL)

Blank-slate book (0 positions). Kill-ledger revisit scan first: NVO/SMFG/TXN were Judge-KILLed 2026-07-13 (judge_kill_cooldown_days=60, live until ~2026-09-11) — excluded from consideration regardless of price even though SMFG (24.95 vs 25.74 entry) and TXN (284.02 vs 296 entry) now sit marginally below their old entry levels: both kills were valuation/EV-math based (SMFG's own honest numbers clear only -3.65%/-4.85% vs ~13% risk-free cash; TXN's own fair-value math computes $85-102/share vs a $284-296 entry/current range), not entry-price based, so a 3-4% pullback does not cure either flaw. No watchlist entries exist. Screen: data/cache/screen_GLOBAL_2026-07-18.csv (24 rows, WASHOUT/MOMENTUM, no NEGLECTED sleeve this week). flow_z60 ignored throughout (GLOBAL foreign-flow layer is vestigial/IDX-only).

### Names re-considered from last week's 2026-07-11 triage (pass reasoning re-checked, not re-litigated from scratch)
- ASML, CAT, MU, AMD, 2020.HK — all still show the SAME structural PASS reason this week (heavy-coverage/consensus-priced, or momentum accelerating further, or thinnest-liquidity-weakest-fundamentals) — momentum in several cases is now MORE extreme (AMD +219% vs +214% prior week; MU +822%), reinforcing rather than reversing the prior pass. Not re-picked.
- MSFT (WASHOUT #2, off -26.89%) — prior pass reason "heaviest coverage in the index, no information edge available" still applies; no new evidence changes this. PASS again.
- NEM (WASHOUT #9, off -31.72%) — prior pass reason "gold-price beta, no company-specific mispricing" still applies; nothing new sources an independent edge this week. PASS again.

### New names this week (not in 2026-07-11 screen)
1. 0700.HK (Tencent, WASHOUT #7, china_growth/em_flows, off -31.07%, mega/heavy) — PICK. Platform quality compounder priced for regulatory/political overhang; genuine differentiated angle vs the US mega-cap tech names already passed on for zero-edge heavy coverage — China-platform consensus dynamics are a different read than US-tech consensus.
2. NOC (Northrop Grumman, WASHOUT #10, global_defense/us_defensive, off -31.79%, moderate coverage) — PICK. Defense prime, moderate (not heaviest) coverage, backlog-driven compounder characteristics under VAL_COMPOUNDER_QUALITY's "any 1-4y compounding thesis" clause; zero sector overlap with anything sourced/killed in the last two weeks.
3. SONY (WASHOUT #6, japan_reflation/us_tech, off -30.2%, heavy coverage, cagr +6.82%) — PICK. Diversified gaming/music/pictures/image-sensor/finance compounder; genuinely under-modeled sum of segments even at heavy headline coverage. Assigned VAL_COMPOUNDER_QUALITY (not HOLDCO) — no dated catalyst confirmed at sourcing stage to satisfy VAL_HOLDCO_DISCOUNT's mandatory-catalyst rule; analyst may surface one during phase-1/2 work, but the default card is the compounder card.
4. TM (Toyota, WASHOUT #4, japan_reflation/us_defensive, off -28.47%) — PASS (close). No clean method-card fit (autos not named under any of the 10 house cards) and japan_reflation factor overlaps SONY; deferred not killed.
5. 1211.HK (BYD, WASHOUT #11, china_growth/nickel_ev, off -33.59%, heavy coverage) — PASS. China EV price-war/margin-compression narrative already heavily covered; no informational edge over consensus.
6. QCOM (WASHOUT #8, semis_ai/us_tech, off -31.31%, moderate coverage) — PASS (close). Sector already had one archetype-mismatch kill this cycle (TXN); a second semis name this week dilutes construction-chain breadth versus the three picks above. First swap-in if any pick falters in dossier stage.
7. PLTR (WASHOUT #12, us_tech/global_defense, off -36.1%, cagr +115.98% despite the pullback) — PASS. Still priced for perfection on any reasonable multiple-survival test even after the drawdown; no margin of safety.
8. 0005.HK (HSBC, MOMENTUM #5, us_rates/china_growth, 0% off 52w high) — PASS. Sitting at a fresh 52-week high with a rate-story thesis already priced in is the same failure pattern that killed SMFG last cycle (policy view already consensus, zero margin of safety) — declining to repeat it on a different bank.
9. UNH, ABBV, OXY, TTE, BP, SHEL (MOMENTUM sleeve) — PASS, uniformly. All sit within ~1-17% of 52-week highs after already-large 12m moves (+18% to +42%); none offer a washout/neglected entry, all are chasing an existing move.

### Final 3 picks for deep-dive (step 7)
- **0700.HK** (WASHOUT) — edge claimed: analytical (SOTP-style segment read + regulatory-overhang normalization vs headline "China platform risk" framing).
- **NOC** (WASHOUT) — edge claimed: analytical (backlog/order-book compounding under-modeled at moderate coverage, no consensus-cycle overlap with anything else sourced this cycle).
- **SONY** (WASHOUT) — edge claimed: analytical (segment sum-of-the-parts under-modeled at heavy headline coverage; gaming/sensor/finance mix priced as one generic "Japan tech" multiple).

All 3 non-MOMENTUM (WASHOUT); rule satisfied trivially. No dossiers/thesis files written at this stage.

Portfolio review (Harrison, PM_GLOBAL) — GLOBAL book: 0 positions, 0% gross, 100% cash, drawdown 0%, governor NONE. No holding-level distance-to-kill/target review applicable (nothing held). No market-value concentration (>15% NAV) possible at 0 names. Nothing to re-affirm this week; construction pipeline (step 7) is this book's only active work.

ADVANCE: MSFT (WASHOUT #2, mega-cap tech, AI-CapEx digestion, EV +8.75%), CAT (MOMENTUM #3, infra pullback, EV +4.0% thin), NEM (WASHOUT #9, gold-miner operational misses, EV +8.75%). NOT CARDED: NVO/TXN/SONY/0700.HK/NOC/SMFG (existing theses), AAPL/ABBV/UNH (no variant perception at levels).

meta.title: WEEKLY_DEEP portfolio review 2026-07-12 — no holdings, pipeline update

body: INDO book: 0 positions, 0% gross, 100% cash (NAV Rp15,011,301,369.86), pre-ramp since inception 2026-07-06 -- nothing to re-affirm, no market-value/target/kill triggers to act on. Foreign-flow feed remains STALE (0 fresh readings) -- flagged, not estimated. This week's 3 deep-dive construction chains (DSNG.JK, ELSA.JK, PNBN.JK) all completed the full pipeline (analyst draft -> Bear attack KILL_RECOMMENDED -> analyst revision -> Bear re-attack reaffirmed -> Judge verdict) and were ALL judged KILL: DSNG.JK (valuation; coverage-lag mechanism empirically refuted by the stock's own post-RUPST bounce, expected_pct -8.9%/3y, no NAV margin of safety), ELSA.JK (process; also uncited 4.0x EV/EBITDA multiple and a conceded sign-inverted archetype, expected_pct -3.8%/3y vs a ~6.5%/yr FR carry), PNBN.JK (valuation; own VAL_BANKS math shows warranted P/BV 0.20-0.27x vs 0.38x traded, expected_pct -24.3%/3y, ~-23.5%/yr equity risk premium vs the FR sovereign sleeve in the same book). A 3-for-3 kill rate in the first real deep-dive batch is notable but reads as the bar working as designed, not evidence the screen is broken -- not lowering the bar to force a trade, and not reaching for the FR sovereign sleeve as a consolation allocation without its own BI-path-anchored thesis. Pipeline continues: next WEEKLY_DEEP resumes sourcing from screen.py + kill_ledger/watchlist revisit scan (all 3 killed tickers now carry documented reason_tags for future revisit-at-lower-price screening).

meta.title: WEEKLY_DEEP portfolio review 2026-07-12 — no holdings, pipeline update

body: GLOBAL book: 0 positions, 0% gross, 100% cash (NAV $1,000,582.19), pre-ramp since inception 2026-07-06 — nothing to re-affirm, no market-value/target/kill triggers to act on. This week's 3 deep-dive construction chains (NVO, SMFG, TXN) all completed the full pipeline (analyst draft -> Bear attack KILL_RECOMMENDED -> analyst revision -> Bear re-attack reaffirmed -> Judge verdict) and were ALL judged KILL: NVO (reason_tag process; merits: analyst's own base-case DCF sat below entry, CagriSema lost its head-to-head to Zepbound, expected_pct -33.5%/3y), SMFG (process; base_rate archetype mismatch + no surviving variant perception, expected_pct -3.65%/3y), TXN (valuation; entry priced ~27-40x own fair EV/EBIT anchor of 12-14x, expected_pct -48.18%/3y). A 3-for-3 kill rate in the first real deep-dive batch is notable but reads as the bar working as designed (each kill is merits-based and independently reproduced by two adversarial passes), not evidence the screen is broken -- not lowering the bar to force a trade. Pipeline continues: next WEEKLY_DEEP resumes sourcing from screen.py + kill_ledger/watchlist revisit scan (all 3 killed tickers now carry documented reason_tags for future revisit-at-lower-price screening).

## Sourcing triage — INDO — 2026-07-11 (Sunda / ANALYST_INDO)

Blank-slate book (0 positions, 0% gross, inception 2026-07-06); no kill_ledger.csv or watchlist revisit candidates exist yet. Screen: data/cache/screen_INDO_2026-07-11.csv (36 rows, WASHOUT/NEGLECTED/MOMENTUM). Foreign-flow feed is STALE (0 fresh readings) — flow_z60 ignored everywhere, including on em_flows-sensitive names (BBCA, BBRI, GOTO) where it would normally matter; flagged as a live blind spot, not a "no signal" read.

### Ranked list (all names seriously considered)
1. DSNG.JK (NEGLECTED, CPO) — PICK. Deepest genuine washout with fundamentals intact (off-high -37.5%, cagr +44.3%); best mispricing-to-liquidity ratio in the CPO cluster.
2. PNBN.JK (WASHOUT, bank) — PICK. Thin-coverage mid-cap bank, real washout but cagr -14.35% — value-trap-or-mispricing question only LK footnotes resolve; VAL_BANKS card at construction.
3. JPFA.JK (WASHOUT/NEGLECTED, poultry) — runner-up, not picked. Thin coverage, washout-then-recovery pattern; first swap-in if DSNG/PNBN falter in dossier stage.
4. SGRO.JK (NEGLECTED, CPO) — PASS. Most extreme washout in the whole screen but ADV ~392mn IDR, roughly 10x thinner than DSNG — monitor-only, real ARA/ARB unsizeability risk.
5. AADI.JK (WASHOUT/MOMENTUM, coal) — PASS. Liquid washout+trend combo, but edge here is macro/China-import-policy, not primary-source digging — MACRO_FX's lane, not this book's this week.
6. ACES.JK (WASHOUT, consumer) — PASS. Deepest cagr decline in WASHOUT but moderate (not thin) coverage — limited informational edge.
7. LSIP.JK (NEGLECTED/MOMENTUM, CPO) — PASS. Thin coverage but least washed-out CPO name; mispricing largely closed vs DSNG/SGRO.
8. RALS.JK (NEGLECTED, consumer) — PASS. Flattish, thin ADV, no compensating mispricing story.
9. AALI.JK (WASHOUT, CPO) — PASS. Positive mom12 makes the WASHOUT tag questionable; weaker than DSNG on drawdown/cagr.
10. BBCA.JK / BBRI.JK (WASHOUT, mega banks) — PASS. Heavy coverage, no thin-coverage edge; also most exposed to the dead flow feed on their primary driver.
11. GOTO.JK (WASHOUT, tech) — PASS. cagr 0.0 (no earnings track record), heavy coverage, Rp50 price makes board-lot value trivial (tick-size cost caveat).
12. GGRM.JK (MOMENTUM, tobacco) — PASS. mom12 +73.8% vs cagr +2.7% — price has vastly outrun fundamentals; re-underwrite/monitor risk, not a fresh long.
13. MAPI.JK (MOMENTUM, consumer) — PASS. At 52w highs with weak cagr relative to the move — overextended.
14. ADRO.JK (MOMENTUM, coal) — PASS. Liquid/trending but heavy coverage, no washout component, no edge.

### Final 3 picks for deep-dive (step 7)
- **DSNG.JK** (NEGLECTED) — edge claimed: thin sell-side coverage on small-cap planter; FFB yield/replanting capex + under-modeled B40/B50 biodiesel mandate demand. Liquidity: ADV 8.6bn IDR, workable but thin — watch ARA/ARB on entry, size conservatively.
- **PNBN.JK** (WASHOUT) — edge claimed: thin coverage on a mid-cap bank with a complex legacy ownership structure; "washout justified or overdone" resolves only via NPL/related-party LK footnotes. Liquidity flag: ADV only 4.0bn IDR — thin, may need multi-day accumulation.
- **ELSA.JK** (cross-listed WASHOUT/NEGLECTED/MOMENTUM, energy services) — edge claimed: thin coverage on state-energy-services-linked name; contract backlog/SKK Migas capex data under-modeled by sell-side. Liquidity: ADV 15.9bn IDR reasonable. Chase-risk flagged: entering after +17.6% 1m move needs dossier to confirm fundamentally justified re-rating, not a flow-driven pop (unverifiable this week given stale flow feed).

≥1 non-MOMENTUM pick satisfied (DSNG.JK NEGLECTED, PNBN.JK WASHOUT). No dossiers/thesis files written at this stage.

## Sourcing triage — GLOBAL — 2026-07-11 (Sloane / ANALYST_GLOBAL)

Blank-slate book (0 positions, 0% gross, inception 2026-07-06); no kill_ledger.csv or watchlist revisit candidates exist yet. Screen: data/cache/screen_GLOBAL_2026-07-11.csv (24 rows, WASHOUT/MOMENTUM, no NEGLECTED sleeve this week). flow_z60 ignored throughout (GLOBAL foreign-flow layer is vestigial/IDX-only).

### Ranked list (all names seriously considered)
1. NVO (WASHOUT) — PICK. -36.8% 12m but +15.6% 1m stabilization; GLP-1 franchise cash generation vs possible over-extrapolated share-loss narrative worth independent document work.
2. SMFG (MOMENTUM) — PICK. Only name at 0% off 52w high with below-average vol; BOJ policy normalization → traceable NIM re-rate, not a chase.
3. TXN (MOMENTUM) — PICK. Most restrained momentum in the sleeve (+32% vs +625% MU); capex/inventory cycle inflection, moderate (not heavy) coverage leaves room for original work.
4. 2318.HK (WASHOUT) — PASS (close). Near-zero bench correlation is a genuine diversifier but Chinese insurer investment-portfolio opacity is a week-one hazard; deferred, not killed.
5. ASML — PASS. Real document trail but heavy coverage means bookings-vs-China-restriction read is already consensus.
6. CAT — PASS. Reflation/infra thesis already in the price; heavy coverage, momentum still accelerating.
7. NEM — PASS. Gold-price beta (51% vol), no company-specific mispricing on offer.
8. MSFT — PASS. Notable drawdown but heaviest coverage in the index; no information edge available.
9. TSM — PASS. Excellent business, zero asymmetry at heavy coverage.
10. 2020.HK — PASS. Thinnest liquidity of WASHOUT top-5, weakest cagr; a China-consumption story not a cash-flow one.
11. AMD — PASS. +214% 12m still accelerating, heavy coverage; poor risk/reward for a first entry.
12. MU — PASS. +625% 12m, 107% vol — a casino, arbitraged many times over.

### Final 3 picks for deep-dive (step 7)
- **NVO** (WASHOUT) — edge claimed: analytical (normalized GLP-1 earnings power vs share-loss narrative).
- **SMFG** (MOMENTUM) — edge claimed: structural-flow (BOJ-driven NIM expansion, policy-mechanical not sentiment).
- **TXN** (MOMENTUM) — edge claimed: analytical (analog semis capex/inventory inflection, under-covered vs AI-momentum peers).

≥1 non-MOMENTUM (WASHOUT) pick satisfied (NVO). No dossiers/thesis files written at this stage.
